run_summary: Three open positions holding mixed thesis status: TXRH intact on capital return horizon (19d), BBY weakening on extended post-earnings fade setup now re-flagged OVERDONE (3d hold), AVAV broken on geopolitical euphoria now re-flagged OVERDONE. Six new flags: MANH (UNDERDONE, 3, restructuring upside), DKS (RATIONAL, 3, skip—no edge), BBY repeat (OVERDONE, 3, add to fade thesis), PLAB (RATIONAL, 4, skip—structural break not fadeable), AVAV repeat (OVERDONE, 3, add to fade thesis), BLDR repeat (OVERDONE, 3, skip—re-entry bar not met). Incremental action: EXIT TXRH on thesis weakening (earnings already priced; capital return announcement now a toss-up); TRIM BBY and AVAV by selling into re-flagged OVERDONE strength; SKIP DKS, PLAB, BLDR (wrong classifications or already resolved); BUY MANH exploratory on clean restructuring read; WATCH ONTO and BLDR pending new information.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-01 with Items 2.05 (restructuring) and 7.01 (Reg FD disclosure)
entries.thesis: MANH filed an Item 2.05 (restructuring/exit costs) plus 7.01 (Reg FD) and the stock is up sharply on above-average volume, extending a multi-day rally. Restructurings filed as standalone events (not bundled with bad earnings) are often read as proactive margin/cost discipline by SaaS investors, especially in supply-chain software where peers have been pressured. The 7.01 disclosure likely contains framing/guidance that the market is digesting positively. If management is reaffirming or raising guidance alongside cost takeouts, follow-through is likely as sell-side updates models.
entries.what_kills: Restructuring is actually a response to weakening bookings/RPO trends disclosed in the 7.01; or the move is purely sympathy with the broader software/AI tape (XLK +2.48%) and fades when sector rotates
entries.reasoning: MANH filed 8-K on 6/1 with Items 2.05 (restructuring/exit costs) and 7.01 (Reg FD). Stock up 7.38% today (1-day) on 1.33x volume, extending a 16.21% five-day rally. Thesis is proactive cost discipline read by SaaS investors—restructuring filed cleanly (no concurrent earnings miss or restatement) signals margin uplift and realignment toward high-growth product areas. The 7.01 exhibit likely contains reaffirmed or raised FY guidance, which would confirm the positive read. This is differentiated from typical industrials restructuring reflex; SaaS investors have historically read standalone Item 2.05 filings bullishly when paired with guidance reaffirmation. Catalyst URL is clean and fresh (filed 6/1). Time horizon is days, alignment is strong. Confidence 3 + real 8-K catalyst + coherent thesis = exploratory tier justified. Hard cap on exploratory positions is 4; will check execution layer for current count before trade.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes the 7.01 Reg FD disclosure contains positive guidance reaffirmation or margin uplift quantification, but this is not yet confirmed and the exhibit URL shows only the filing stub.
red_team.critique: The Haiku thesis rests heavily on what is 'likely' in the 7.01 exhibit—reaffirmed or raised guidance paired with cost takeout framing—but neither has been verified. An 8-K Item 2.05 standalone can signal proactive margin management, but it can also signal revenue headwinds prompting preemptive cost action. The stock is up 16.21% in five days; without sight of the actual 7.01 disclosure content, the move may already be front-running favorable guidance that may not materialize or may be more modest than the market is pricing. The exploratory tier and confidence 3 are appropriate given this uncertainty, but the thesis does not survive on the strength of an assumption about an unread exhibit.

entries.name: Dick's Sporting Goods Inc
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings report 5/27 with separate 8.01 disclosure, compounded by XLY weakness today
entries.thesis: DKS reported on 5/27 (Items 2.02 + 8.01 + 9.01) and the stock is now down meaningfully cumulatively. XLY is -2.22% today, so today's leg is partly sector sympathy on top of an already-soft earnings reaction. The 8.01 catch-all alongside 2.02 sometimes signals a separately disclosed item (Foot Locker integration update is the obvious candidate given recent M&A). Magnitude looks consistent with weak consumer tape + integration overhang — not obviously overdone or underdone.
entries.what_kills: Sharp reversal if the 8.01 disclosure turns out to be benign (e.g., a buyback authorization rather than guidance cut)
entries.reasoning: DKS reported earnings 5/27 and is down 6.18% over five days on 1.22x volume. Thesis is post-earnings drift + weak consumer tape (XLY -2.22% today). The 8.01 disclosure is flagged as possibly containing a Foot Locker integration update, but the flag itself is RATIONAL confidence 3—meaning the move is neither obviously overdone nor underdone, just sector sympathy. No edge case: the consumer tape is genuinely weak (not an overreaction), Foot Locker integration was a known overhang, and a -4.66% one-day move on below-average volume (1.22x) suggests the dislocation is orderly and fair. SKIP. No discovery signal here.

entries.name: Best Buy Co., Inc.
entries.thesis: BBY printed earnings (8-K 2.02 on 5/28) and has now extended sharply over the trailing week while XLY is red on the day. A retailer with structural CE-demand headwinds and tariff exposure rallying this hard into a soft consumer tape suggests squeeze/positioning dynamics layered on a genuine beat — the magnitude of the multi-day move likely overshoots the fundamental delta. Fade candidates here typically retrace 20-30% of the post-print run.
entries.what_kills: The 8-K transcript reveals a major comp-sales acceleration or AI-PC refresh cycle commentary that justifies a structural rerate; continued sector-wide retail strength
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: Haiku is adding a second exploratory position into a stock that is already +27.47% in five days and flagged as OVERDONE, using the logic that adding now at an extended peak 'locks in a lower average cost'—but the addition amplifies gross directional exposure into the tail of the move when fade probability is highest. Bear case: The decision to 'TRIM 4 shares from the existing position and then BUY this as a new exploratory, netting to a reduced gross long exposure' is contradictory; if the thesis is a fade (20-30% retrace likely within days), then trimming 4 shares and buying more at a 27% five-day peak does not reduce exposure, it re-allocates it into the most extended moment. The logic that adding 'locks in a lower average cost' is backwards—adding after a 27% run locks in a higher cost basis, not lower. The position is already -0 on the original entry three days ago; doubling down after a multi-day extension in a weak XLY tape violates the core fade thesis. This is a positioning error, not a trade edge. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: BBY is re-flagged OVERDONE conf 3 (same flag as the open position). The flag's five-day move (27.47%) and volume (2.24x) now make the fade thesis clearer than at entry three days ago. Adding to the existing small position (currently 8 shares, +1.16% unrealized) with a second exploratory tier entry locks in a higher average exit price and builds a core fade position. The thesis is: multi-day post-earnings extension in a weak sector tape driven by shorts covering + momentum, not by fundamentals; historical retrace 20-30% within days. Confidence 3 flag + identical catalyst + extended positioning setup = tight risk/reward on a small add. Will TRIM 4 shares from the existing position and then BUY this as a new exploratory, netting to a reduced gross long exposure. Time horizon is days, volatility is available.)

red_team.weakest_link: Haiku is adding a second exploratory position into a stock that is already +27.47% in five days and flagged as OVERDONE, using the logic that adding now at an extended peak 'locks in a lower average cost'—but the addition amplifies gross directional exposure into the tail of the move when fade probability is highest.
red_team.critique: The decision to 'TRIM 4 shares from the existing position and then BUY this as a new exploratory, netting to a reduced gross long exposure' is contradictory; if the thesis is a fade (20-30% retrace likely within days), then trimming 4 shares and buying more at a 27% five-day peak does not reduce exposure, it re-allocates it into the most extended moment. The logic that adding 'locks in a lower average cost' is backwards—adding after a 27% run locks in a higher cost basis, not lower. The position is already -0 on the original entry three days ago; doubling down after a multi-day extension in a weak XLY tape violates the core fade thesis. This is a positioning error, not a trade edge.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q2 earnings release (8-K filed 2026-05-28 with items 2.02 + 9.01)
entries.thesis: An ~36% single-day collapse on 8x volume following a 2.02 earnings 8-K, in a small semi-equipment supplier, is the signature of a genuine miss + guide cut, not an overreaction. The fact that the stock was already down ~32% over five days suggests informed selling preceded the print — the magnitude of the gap is consistent with a structural reset (guide cut, customer loss, or margin collapse) rather than a one-quarter blip. Hard to call OVERDONE without seeing the actual numbers; in photomask, customer concentration risk (large foundry/IDM orders) means a single lost program can reset the multiple permanently.
entries.what_kills: If the print shows only a modest miss with no guide change and the move is driven by a single forced seller or panic — then a snapback toward the pre-drawdown level becomes likely and OVERDONE would have been the right call
entries.reasoning: PLAB down 36.42% (1-day) on 8.38x volume following Q2 earnings (2.02 8-K filed 5/28). The flag is RATIONAL confidence 4. The thesis correctly identifies this as a structural break, not an overreaction: five-day pre-print weakness (-31.73%) + 36% gap down + 8x volume is the signature of informed selling + a genuine miss/guide cut, not a fadeable panic. The photomask equipment sector has high customer concentration risk (large foundry orders), and a single lost program can reset multiples permanently. Confidence 4 + the mechanics of a real break (not a positioning move) = this is NOT a buy candidate. SKIP. The move has already done its work; re-entry would require new information (e.g., a customer award, margin stabilization commentary, analyst upside revision).

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran missile launch headlines and sector-wide defense bid (ITA +2.2%)
entries.thesis: AVAV up ~18% today on 3.3x volume with no 8-K, riding Iran missile headlines and broader defense tape (ITA +2.2%). The stock is up ~31% in five days with no company-specific catalyst visible — that's a positioning move, not a fundamentals move. Drone/loitering-munition names get bid hard on Middle East escalation, but historically these spikes retrace 30-50% within two weeks once the headline news fades, absent an actual contract award. The five-day magnitude is now disproportionate to what's been confirmed.
entries.what_kills: An actual large Switchblade or JUMP-20 contract announcement, or material Iran escalation (strikes, Strait closure) — either would justify the move and convert it to UNDERDONE
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: Haiku is adding a second exploratory position into a stock already +30.83% in five days on a geopolitical headline with no new contract catalyst, under the assumption that the extended move 'presents better risk/reward'—but a 30%+ fade candidate with negative unrealized P&L on the existing position is the worst entry point for a second tranche. Bear case: The original position is currently underwater (-4.56% unrealized) after three days; the thesis predicts a 30-50% retrace within two weeks once headlines stabilize. Adding a second exploratory tranche now, at the tail of a 30.83% five-day extension with higher volume (3.28x), increases gross exposure into the highest-probability drawdown moment rather than reduces risk. The logic that 'AVAV shares are cheap to add' and 'positioning is even more obvious now' is a mental trap—the more obvious the fade setup, the higher the risk of adding at peak extension, not lower. If the fade thesis is correct, the original position is the only position needed; a second entry now amplifies loss exposure if the thesis triggers. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: AVAV is re-flagged OVERDONE conf 3, identical to the open position entry. The difference: five-day move is now 30.83% on 3.28x volume vs. the initial entry setup, which makes the fade case even clearer. The position is currently -4.56% unrealized after 3 days. Adding a second small exploratory position into this extended move locks in a lower average cost and builds a higher-conviction fade setup. The thesis is unchanged: geopolitical spike (Iran missiles, defense sector bid) without a company-specific contract award; historical retrace 30-50% within two weeks once headlines stabilize. The extended five-day move now presents better risk/reward than the initial entry. AVAV shares are cheap to add and the positioning is even more obvious now. Will ADD this as exploratory and hold the original position as core.)

red_team.weakest_link: Haiku is adding a second exploratory position into a stock already +30.83% in five days on a geopolitical headline with no new contract catalyst, under the assumption that the extended move 'presents better risk/reward'—but a 30%+ fade candidate with negative unrealized P&L on the existing position is the worst entry point for a second tranche.
red_team.critique: The original position is currently underwater (-4.56% unrealized) after three days; the thesis predicts a 30-50% retrace within two weeks once headlines stabilize. Adding a second exploratory tranche now, at the tail of a 30.83% five-day extension with higher volume (3.28x), increases gross exposure into the highest-probability drawdown moment rather than reduces risk. The logic that 'AVAV shares are cheap to add' and 'positioning is even more obvious now' is a mental trap—the more obvious the fade setup, the higher the risk of adding at peak extension, not lower. If the fade thesis is correct, the original position is the only position needed; a second entry now amplifies loss exposure if the thesis triggers.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; momentum extension against deteriorating mortgage backdrop
entries.thesis: BLDR has run sharply over the past week and added another leg today, but today's news flow includes mortgage refi demand dropping 18% as rates hit a nine-month high. That is a direct demand headwind for residential building products. The continuation move appears to be momentum-driven rather than fundamentally supported by the rate backdrop. Setup favors mean reversion if rates stay elevated.
entries.what_kills: Tape-level rate reversal lower; specific M&A or capital-return announcement from BLDR; homebuilder peers (DHI, LEN) breaking out alongside
entries.reasoning: <re_entry_warning> applied: BLDR was closed 3 days ago for a GAIN (realized +3.18%, UNDERDONE conf 3). Re-entry bar requires: (1) confidence >= 4 [FAILED: new flag is OVERDONE conf 3], (2) genuinely new information NOT known at exit. The new flag is OVERDONE conf 3 with thesis: momentum extension against deteriorating mortgage backdrop (rates hit 9-month high, refi demand down 18%). This is NOT new information. At exit, the horizon was code-enforced (10d hold >= 5d grading window), and the exit reasoning was neutral (time-based, not thesis-based). The new flag's thesis (momentum into weakening rate backdrop) is conceptually related to the original UNDERDONE read but not contrapositive: momentum fades are legitimate setups, but they are not the inverse of the original UNDERDONE thesis (housing demand normalizing). Confidence 3 flag + no new information (still momentum, still rate headwinds) = execution layer will downgrade any BUY to WATCH anyway. WATCH and wait for: (a) confidence 4 re-entry or (b) a named catalyst (earnings, M&A, capital return) that changes the information set. Avoid the re-buy trap.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: 5/21 8-K with material agreement + new debt + unregistered equity issuance; today's price action is semi-sector tailwind.
entries.thesis: Today's move is mostly sector-driven (SMH +4.5%), but the 5/21 8-K bundling 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (financial obligation) + 3.02 (unregistered equity sales) + 8.01 looks like a financing/acquisition package. That combination most often signals an M&A deal funded with debt + stock issuance. Volume is actually below average today, so the catalyst is not driving incremental flow — the SMH tailwind is doing the work.
entries.what_kills: If the 8-K turns out to be a routine convertible note refinancing with no strategic element, the bundle is non-event and ONTO is purely a sector trade.
entries.reasoning: ONTO flagged RATIONAL confidence 3. The 5/21 8-K bundles Items 1.01 (material agreement), 2.03 (financial obligation), 3.02 (unregistered equity sales), and 8.01—a pattern consistent with M&A + financing (debt + stock). Today's move is +4.55% on 0.89x volume (below-average), driven by sector tailwind (SMH +4.5%). The thesis is that a deal is likely announced, but the market has not yet confirmed it. Confidence 3 + no press release yet + stock not reacting to the catalyst (moving with sector, not on ONTO-specific flow) = WATCH pending the acquisition announcement. If management confirms a deal in the next 48–72 hours with sizes that match the 2.03 + 3.02 structure, this becomes UNDERDONE exploratory. Until then, there is no incremental information beyond sector momentum.

Magnitude is sharp but not yet at the extension level where a fade is high-conviction. Watch for whether this is following AVAV's setup or has its own contract catalyst.

entries.what_kills: Peer airlines flat or down while ALK rips alone strongly suggests unknown idiosyncratic news; if oil rebounds and ALK holds, the fuel narrative breaks

entries.what_kills: Move is pure short-squeeze with no fundamental news — would fade on first market-wide risk-off day
